titleStmt.title: Letter from William French to Sir Matthew Nathan, 17 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Joseph French

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from William J. French toSir Matthew Nathan referring to the allowance paid by French to his under sheriff every six months. French has supplied a receipt for the payment and requests to be reimbursed.William Joseph French (1885-1974) served as High sheriff of Roscommon in 1915. He also fought in the First World War with the 6th Battalion, Worcestershire Regiment. He would later go on to fight again in the Second World War as a captain.Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) served as the under secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers,, CSO RP/1916/5596/164/15

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Sheriff's Office, ROSCOMMON. 17th December 1915. Sir, I beg to acknowledge receipt of yours of
the 16th inst for which I am obliged, and as requested
send herewith Voucher for second Moiety of my
Under Sheriff's Salary Â£75:0:0, and shall be thankful
for Paying Order for amount. I have the honour to remain,
Your obedient servant, William J French
High Sheriff. The Right Hon The Under Secretary for Ireland, Dublin Castle.
